start-coding.meta.title: Start Coding with AI - DevAI Day Workshop
start-coding.meta.description: Learn IDE-based GitHub Copilot features through hands-on exercises. Master code completion, Copilot Chat, agent mode, and more.
start-coding.sidebar.title: Start Coding with AI
start-coding.progress.label: Progress

start-coding.step2.title: Code Completion
start-coding.step3.title: Copilot Chat

start-coding.step1.p1: You can use your own repository or work with the sample repository available at:
start-coding.step1.forkIntro: First, open the project URL above in your browser and fork the repository:
start-coding.step1.forkLi1: Open the project URL in your browser
start-coding.step1.forkLi2: Click the Fork button in the top right
start-coding.step1.forkCaption: Click the Fork button to create your own copy of the repository
start-coding.step1.forkLi3: Click the Create fork button on the fork creation screen. Once the fork is complete, a copy of the repository will be created in your GitHub account.
start-coding.step1.envIntro: Using your forked repository, you can start the project using VS Code:
start-coding.step1.localIntro: If you have VSCode installed locally:
start-coding.step1.cloneLi2: Clone your forked repository with the following command:
start-coding.step1.workspaceIntro: After opening the project, please install the following extensions (if you haven't):
start-coding.step1.workspaceLi2: Login to GitHub Copilot using your GitHub account
start-coding.step2.p1: GitHub Copilot provides intelligent code suggestions as you type. Let's explore how to use this feature effectively.
start-coding.step2.p2: Create a new JavaScript file named factorial.js in your project.
start-coding.step2.p3: As you write code, Copilot analyzes the context of your file and suggests completions. These suggestions appear as gray "ghost text" that you can accept with the <kbd>Tab</kbd> key.
start-coding.step2.functionPrompt: Add a New Function Using a Prompt: Start typing a comment describing what you want:
start-coding.step2.functionResult: Copilot will automatically suggest the full function implementation as ghost text.
start-coding.step2.classPrompt: Add a New Class Using a Prompt: Begin describing a class structure:
start-coding.step2.classResult: Copilot will generate the class definition and related methods.
start-coding.step2.helperResult: Copilot will propose a ready-to-use helper function.
start-coding.step2.incompleteResult: Copilot will expand the validation logic and complete the rest of the function.
start-coding.step2.docsResult: Copilot will auto-generate a documentation block.
start-coding.step2.note: If you don't see suggestions, make sure you're signed in to your GitHub account and have an active Copilot subscription.
start-coding.step3.p1: Copilot Chat is an interactive AI assistant that helps you understand code, generate functions, and get coding guidance through natural language conversations.
start-coding.step3.openLi1: Click the Chat icon (chat bubble) in the VSCode sidebar
start-coding.step3.openLi2: Or open the Chat panel with <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>Alt</kbd>+<kbd>I</kbd> (macOS: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>Cmd</kbd>+<kbd>I</kbd>)
start-coding.step3.chatCaption: The Copilot Chat panel in VS Code
start-coding.step3.explainP1: Option 1: Select code and ask Copilot to explain it:
start-coding.step3.explainCaption: Copilot provides detailed explanations of your code
start-coding.step3.explainP2: Option 2: Select code and ask Copilot to explain it using slash commands:
start-coding.step3.generateP: Ask Copilot to write code for you:
start-coding.step3.translateP: Convert code from one language or framework to another:
start-coding.step3.improveP: Get suggestions to improve your code quality:
start-coding.step3.tip: Use the # symbol to reference files in your prompts, like #app.py to give Copilot context about specific files.
start-coding.step4.p1: Agent Mode is Copilot's most autonomous feature. It can execute multi-step tasks, run commands, and make complex changes across your project.
start-coding.step4.diffLi1: Autonomous Execution: Copilot can run terminal commands, create files, and iterate on solutions
start-coding.step4.diffLi2: Error Recovery: When errors occur, Agent Mode attempts to fix them automatically
start-coding.step4.diffLi3: Multi-file Operations: Can modify many files as part of a single task
start-coding.step4.enableLi2: Select Agent from the mode selector at the bottom of the chat panel
start-coding.step4.agentCaption: Agent Mode working autonomously on a coding task
start-coding.step4.caution: Agent Mode can run commands and modify files. Always review the actions it proposes before allowing them to execute.
start-coding.step5.p1: Let's build a complete enterprise-ready application from scratch using Copilot. We'll create an Employee Management System with CRUD operations.
start-coding.step5.requirementsP: Start by providing Copilot with a clear requirement prompt for an enterprise application:
start-coding.step5.structureP: Ask Copilot to generate the application structure based on your preferred tech stack:
start-coding.step5.mockTitle: Optional: Add UI from Mock or Drawing
start-coding.step5.mockP: If you have a UI mockup or wireframe, you can attach it to help Copilot generate matching UI components:
start-coding.step5.mockLi1: Attach your UI mockup image in Copilot Chat
start-coding.step5.tip: You can use hand-drawn sketches or wireframe tools like Figma, Balsamiq, or even simple diagrams to guide Copilot's UI generation.
start-coding.step5.saveP: Use Agent Mode to save the architecture to a file:
start-coding.step5.archCaption: Architecture documentation is now part of your project
start-coding.step6.p1: Plan Mode helps you create a structured development plan before implementation. This is part of Spec-Driven Development, a recommended approach for AI-assisted coding.
start-coding.step6.planCaption: Switch to Plan mode for structured planning
start-coding.step6.tip: If there are points that should be improved, point them out! For example: "Considering the ease of unit testing, please also list any improvements or additions needed to the current plan."
start-coding.step6.saveP: Switch to Agent mode and save the plan:
start-coding.step6.resultCaption: Your development plan is now documented
start-coding.step6.p2: Now you have two specification files: architecture.md and plan.md. These will guide Copilot during implementation.
start-coding.step7.p1: Now let's implement the application using Copilot's Agent mode with our specification documents.
start-coding.step7.instructionsP: Before implementing, set up custom instructions to guide Copilot:
start-coding.step7.instructionsCaption: Custom instructions help Copilot understand your project conventions
start-coding.step7.promptsP: For reusable prompts, create prompt files at .github/prompts/*.prompt.md:
start-coding.step7.promptsCaption: Prompt files provide reusable, structured prompts
start-coding.step7.startP: Attach the plan.md file and use this prompt:
start-coding.step7.agentCaption: Copilot implements the application following the plan
start-coding.step7.verifyLi1: Directory Structure: Is it structured according to the recommended architecture?
start-coding.step7.verifyLi2: Basic Files: Are necessary files (app.py, HTML templates, CSS files, etc.) created?
start-coding.step7.verifyLi3: Operation Check: Run simple tests to verify functionality
start-coding.step8.p1: Let's use Copilot to add comprehensive unit tests to our application.
start-coding.step8.generateP: Use the following prompt to have Copilot create unit tests:
start-coding.step8.runningP: You can ask Copilot to run the tests directly from the Chat panel:
start-coding.step8.resultsCaption: Unit test results in the terminal
start-coding.step8.tip: Copilot can automatically check test results and make adjustments if tests fail. It uses your custom instructions and architecture document to ensure consistency.
start-coding.step9.p1: Well-documented code is essential for maintainability. Let's use Copilot to generate comprehensive documentation.
start-coding.step9.generateP: Use this prompt to create detailed documentation:
start-coding.step9.mermaidCaption: Documentation includes Mermaid diagrams for visual representation
start-coding.step10.p1: Custom Agents allow you to create specialized AI assistants for specific tasks in your project. This is an optional but powerful feature to enhance your workflow.
start-coding.step10.whatP: Custom Agents are specialized AI assistants you can configure for specific tasks. They can be scoped to particular file types, coding patterns, or documentation tasks.
start-coding.step10.createP: You can create custom agents for specific tasks. For example, a "README creator" agent:
start-coding.step10.moreP: Here are some other useful custom agents you can create:
start-coding.step10.tip: Refer to the Awesome Copilot Repository for more custom agent examples and best practices.
start-coding.step10.congratsP: You've completed the "Start Coding with AI" learning path! You've learned:
start-coding.step10.learnLi1: Using specifications to develop an application
start-coding.step10.learnLi5: Creating custom agents for specialized tasks
start-coding.step10.nextLi1: Try using Copilot in your own projects
start-coding.step10.nextLi3: Explore the Copilot Pro Features learning path
